title: danthonia raoulii
intro: Danthonia raoulii, also known as Raoule's danthonia, is a species of grass native to New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and relieve pain. The health benefits of Danthonia raoulii include its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can aid in the healing of skin infections and other microbial-related conditions. Danthonia raoulii can be prepared as a tea or infusion for medicinal use, and it has also been traditionally used in poultices and compresses to apply its healing properties to the skin.
